sections.paragraph:
Νίγερ, ὁ (GDI 1555c, 20; e, 24 [Phocis]; ostracon APF 6, 1920, 213, 1 [174/75 a.d.] Αἰβύτιος Νίγερ; Sb 46; 8808, 3 [imp. period]; O. Wilck II, 266; 296; wood tablet w. the name of the veteran L. Petronius Niger [94 a.d.] in Dssm., LO 383 [LAE 443]; Jos., Bell. 2, 520) Niger (cp. Lat. niger=dark-complexioned), surname of Simeon the prophet Ac 13:1.—M-M.

GDI GDI = Sammlung der griechischen Dialekt-Inschriften—List 3

APF APF = Archiv für Papyrusforschung—Lists 4, 6

Sb Sb = Sammelbuch griechischer Urkunden aus Aegypten—Lists 3, 4

imp. imp. = imperial

  1. Wilck O. Wilck = UWilcken, ed., Griechische Ostraka aus Ägypten u. Nubien—List 4
  2. L. = KLachmann, editor of the Greek NT (1842–50)—List 1, beg.

Dssm. Dssm. = ADeissmann, various works—List 6

LO LO = Licht vom Osten—s. Dssm., LO in List 6

LAE LAE , s. Dssm. LO—List 6

Jos. Jos. = Josephus. This abbr. used when follow by title; I a.d.—Lists 5

  1. cp. = compare, freq. in ref. to citation fr. ancient texts

Lat. Lat. = Latin

M-M M-M = JMoulton/GMilligan, Vocabulary of Greek Testament—Lists 4, 6

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 672
